title: thymus pulegioides var. istriacus
intro: Thymus pulegioides var. istriacus, also known as Istrian thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus pulegioides plant, a type of herbaceous perennial plant belonging to the mint family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Istrian thyme include reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and treating skin conditions such as acne and wounds. In herbal preparations, Istrian thyme can be used in teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be applied topically or inhaled to reap its medicinal and therapeutic benefits.
